rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DAVID L. K. HUANG REVOCABLE TRUST UTD, 09/14/2016
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. The trustee has not yet addressed the issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 12/12/22. 2. Additionally, it appears that not all of her time sheets were attached to her accounting petition. The last time sheet attached to the petition was for the month of October 2020. The time sheets for the months of October 2019 through September 2020 total 881.5 hours, not 1,701 hours, as alleged in paragraph 11. 3. Trustee does not specifically request that she be allowed to continue to pay herself $3,500/month on account of her compensation. 4. Trustee requests compensation based on 1% of the FMV at the end of this two-year accounting period less the amount paid to a third party to prepare the accounting, but she also pays Robert Kearns $1,000/month to help manage real property. 5. The time sheets indicate that Trustee apparently often eats meals with the settlor/beneficiary that the trust purchases. 6. Accounting for the two-year period ending 9/30/23 will be delinquent by early December. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SANDRA MARGARITA ESPINOZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. In January 2023, the Court issued an order identifying Josefina Cupido as a beneficiary and distributee entitled to 20% of the estate. The Court's January 2023 order determining Josefina's entitlement and share has become final, binding, and conclusive under Probate Code section 11705. Nevertheless, the petition requests distribution of Josefina's 20% share to her child Ronald Cupido, one of the petitioners and co-administrators, based on Josefina's purported disclaimer of her interest in this estate. The disclaimer was not filed or otherwise disclosed before the Court's January 2023 order. The disclaimer was signed by Ronald as agent under Josefina's power of attorney. The power of attorney is defective on its face, however, as the notary public failed to include declaration language required by Civil Code section 1189(a)(3). Moreover, the power of attorney did not expressly grant the power to disclaim Josefina's interest in this estate. See section 4264(d). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROY ELLIS KUTZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. If there are 20 shares of General Motors, 14 shares of Morgan Stanley Dean Witter, and 12 shares of Dean Witter, Discover & Co. on hand, each of the two heirs should receive only half the number of shares. This was corrected in the "supplement" filed on 9/5/23 but then reverted in this amended petition to propose distribution of the full number of shares of each of these three stocks to both heirs. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESUSITA A. DOMATOL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/18/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) When the petition seeks a non-pro rata distribution, it must show the computation on which the proposed distribution is based. Consents of interested beneficiaries must be filed. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.a.ii.] (2) Detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sale must be filed to support the statutory fee. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] (3) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or BENEFICIARY has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (4) If the cash on hand totals $1,012,293.15, the net amount shown in paragraph 8 of the prayer appears to be incorrect. (5) The other attorney did not also sign this petition and she was not given notice. Was there an agreement to split the statutory fee equally? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Social Security numbers should be redacted on papers filed with the Court.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALZINA MARIE RAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Distribute Share Of Estate Of Heir Marcus Mcclure To Special Needs Trust For His Benefit
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues (see tentative rulings for past 3 hearings) remain unaddresed since the initial hearing on 08/21/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. A copy of the proposed trust must be attached to the order. LRSF 14.48(B). 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) Please provide a specific description and value of each asset distributable to Marcus McClure from this estate, together with annual income from each asset, for the purpose of setting the trustee's initial bond. 4) No guardian ad litem has been appointed for Marcus McClure. Does he qualify as a person who lacks legal capacity to make decisions within the meaning of Probate Code section 1003(a)(2)? 5) A budget of anticipated expenditures to be paid out of the proposed trust must be provided. LRSF 14.49(B). 6) Please identify all public benefits received by Marcus McClure. 7) Please identify issue of Marcus McClure. If he has no issue, please identify his heirs. 8) Does the petitioner, if appointed as trustee, intend to carry on the day-to-day activity of the trust in the county where the petitioner resides? If so, should the Court transfer jurisdiction to the county of the petitioner's residence per section 3604(a)(1)? 9) If there is no conservatorship case, a petition to establish a special needs trust must be filed in a new trust case which must be opened under the name of the proposed trust. Note: If Marcus McClure lacks legal capacity, the Court is to determine whether to override the bond waiver purportedly signed by Marcus McClure and require the administrator to give a full bond ($975,000 equal to the final inventory and appraisal value, although notice of proposed action indicates that the estate real property may have been sold for $1,000,000). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM DORSEY STUART JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Mutual General Release
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/17/2024 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 11/20/2023). The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No stipulation or joinder signed by Nikki and Ricardo required by section 4 of the settlement agreement is on file. 3) Nikki has already dismissed her petition for probate with prejudice. Is the "pending litigation" requirement of CCP section 664.6 satisfied to allow the Court to retain jurisdiction to enforce the settlement? See Sayta v. Chu (2017) 17 Cal.App.5th 960. If the petitioner's will contest should qualify as pending litigation, many interested persons, including California Pacific Medical Foundation, have not been served in the manner required by Probate Code section 8250(a) and as such may not be bound by the settlement. See section 8251(c)(3).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM DORSEY STUART JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/17/2024 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 11/20/2023). The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 4) Item 1 of the DE-121 form must specify all names by which the decedent was known, including William Dorsey Stuart, W. Dorsey Stuart, Bill Stuart, and Dorsey Stuart (see DE-121 forms previously filed by Nikki Balgos). New notice must be mailed and published. 5) Legal and factual bases must be alleged in support of an implied finding that the decedent's 2022 will is invalid in its entirety and did not operate to revoke the 2012 will. Note: The will contest previously filed by the petitioner only challenged the provisions of the 2022 will purporting to make donative transfers to Nikki Balgos and Richard Maglaya under Probate Code section 21380. While Nikki Balgos and Richard Maglaya would be deemed to have predeceased the decedent if the provisions failed under section 21386, the validity of the remainder of the 2022 will does not appear to be affected. If so, should the 2022 will be admitted to probate, subject to assignments of the shares of Nikki Balgos and Richard Maglaya in accordance with the settlement agreement sought to be approved by the Court, although California Pacific Medical Foundation entitled to 10% of the residue under the 2022 will is not a party to the settlement agreement and has not assigned its shares.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE EDWARD M. DOWD 1997 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Of Adrew Koch To Determine Legitamacy Of Heirs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/8/24. (No earlier availability.) Other than a copy of the trust, no evidence has been provided to support Petitioner's allegations. (Among other things, Petitioner does not state his date of birth. A copy of the DNA test was not attached to the petition. If the trustor must have also submitted his DNA to the ancestry.com database but then amended his trust on 2/24/22, just three days before he died and in which trust he intentionally omitted to provide for any of his heirs, how does Petitioner conclude that Trustor would have provided for him?) Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/26/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A "Response to Probate Examiner Notes", particularly by a party other than the petitioner, when the issues pertained to the petition, is never an appropriate pleading. A party objecting to a petition must file objections. In any event, it is not necessary to recite or quote a tentative ruling; a pleading should stand on its own and not "answer" issues posted by an examiner. The petition filed in the prior decedent's estate case is no longer before the Court.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PINKEY BROUSSARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Leslie M. Byrne's Petition Continued to 12/18/23. (No earlier availability.) (1) If Petitioner is the successor trustee of Rita's trust, she must file proof of her standing as such. (2) No personal representative has been appointed yet for the post-deceased granddaughter's estate. (3) Notice will have to be given to that personal representative, and if it is also this petitioner, all beneficiaries of Pinkey Franklin Gibson's estate will also have to be identified and be given notice. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PINKEY BROUSSARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Cynthia's Corrected Petition Continued to 12/18/23. (No earlier availability.) (1) No proof of service or proof of publication of the CORRECTED petition has been filed yet. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) No personal representatives have been appointed yet for the post-deceased daughter's and granddaughter's estates. Notice cannot be given to an "estate". Until their successors-in-interest are ascertained, this estate cannot proceed. (4) Are Robert Gibson and Tanya Gibson the issue of granddaughter Pinkey Franklin Gibson? (5) Notice mailed to more than one person in the same household must be sent separately to each person. [CRC 7.51(a)(3).] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JIAJU LI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of publication has been filed yet. (2) Mailed notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (4) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/11/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
